creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
Places I wouldn’t travel to again 👇
 Beauty is everywhere, but sometimes, things don’t click when visiting a new destination. Here are a few of the places I’ve travelled that I’m not in a rush to re-visit 🫣
 Naples, Italy 🇮🇹 Everybody I spoke to before visiting Naples told me it was filthy, a bit dodgy and that they didn’t feel welcome as tourists. I was eager to prove them wrong and I had high hopes for Naples. But after spending a full day walking the streets of the city centre and the Spanish Quarter I had to admit defeat: my friends were right. Still, the pizzas I had in Naples were the best I’ve ever had, but that’s about the only positive thing I can say about this city.  Birmingham, England 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿 I’m a massive sucker for all things British and I’ve got my love goggles on every time I’m visiting new places in the UK, but Birmingham… How is the 2nd largest city in England so incredibly boring? There were some good places in Digbeth and the Jewelry Quarter, but I’d expect more for a city of this size. Cities like Glasgow, Manchester, Liverpool, Leeds and Brighton are 10x more interesting.

Dublin, Ireland 🇮🇪 I really wanted to like Dublin so it confused me a bit when I, after spending a couple of days in the Irish capital, just thought it was a bit dull. I found people a bit unfriendly, which surprised me, and I struggled to find interesting things to do. When “visit the university” is one of the top suggestions from locals, you know it’s time to get out of the city. I will 100% be back in Ireland and explore the beautiful nature and smaller cities.

Have you visited any of these destinations? Let me know your thoughts in a comment, and I’m also interested in hearing about the places you’ve visited and wouldn’t return to ✈️

#travel #naples #birmingham #dublin

transcription.text: Oh, get me away from here, I'm dying
